subject: Chemistry
title: Stainless Steel Won't Rust
description: One-minute skill: Stainless Steel Won't Rust.

hook.text: Add chromium to steel and it becomes 'stainless'—resistant to rust, stains, and corrosion.

buildup.text: Normal steel rusts because iron reacts with oxygen and water to form flaky iron oxide.

discovery.text: Chromium forms a thin, invisible oxide layer on the surface that blocks oxygen from reaching the iron beneath.

twist.text: Scratch stainless steel and the chromium oxide layer heals itself within seconds. It's self-repairing.

climax.text: Harry Brearley invented it in 1913 while trying to make better gun barrels. He noticed the discarded steel didn't rust.

punchline.text: Chromium creates an invisible shield that heals itself. Rust can't get in.

quiz.question: How does chromium make steel stainless?
options: It forms a self-healing oxide layer that blocks oxygen from reaching iron
It makes the steel too hard for oxygen to penetrate
It absorbs all moisture from the air around it
